title: [InstCombine] Don't cover up poison elements for shifts when folding shuffles thru binops
summary:
The developers generalized the optimization by removing an overly restrictive safety check for shift operations. Originally, the transform prevented folding when a vector constant contained poison lanes and the operation was a shift with a constant operand (`ConstOp1`). This blocked valid optimizations for `ashr`, `lshr`, and `shl` with splat shuffles.

The fix recognizes that shifts, unlike integer division/remainder, do not invoke immediate undefined behavior from poison lanes in the constant operand. Poison lanes propagate safely through shifts without introducing new UB. Thus, the safety masking (`getSafeVectorConstantForBinop`) is only necessary for `isIntDivRem()`.

This change enables the pattern to apply broadly: any shift with a constant vector (splat or partial) can now fold with a preceding shuffle, reducing instructions. The generalization is minimal—removing a single condition—but unlocks multiple related optimizations, as shown in the test expansions. When generalizing, identify which operations truly require poison handling versus those where poison propagation is safe.
